topic.title: J.K. Rowling — 12 rejections before Harry Potter
topic.context: Single mother on welfare in Edinburgh, 1990-1997. Rejected by Bloomsbury 12 times before an 8-year-old daughter of the CEO championed the book. Focus on the specific mechanics of rejection and word-of-mouth breakthrough. Avoid unverified quotes.

script.title_working: How She Turned Rejection Into a Global Movement
script.hook: She was rejected 30 times before one yes changed everything.

slides.heading: Thirty Doors Slammed Shut
slides.body: One dream. Thirty rejections. Zero backup plan.
slides.narration: Before J.K. Rowling became a household name, twelve publishers rejected the first Harry Potter book. She was a single mother on welfare, writing in cafes.
slides.stock_query: person writing cafe cinematic uplifting real people

slides.heading: The Yes That Almost Wasn't
slides.body: A publisher's 8-year-old daughter said yes for him.
slides.narration: Bloomsbury only agreed to publish after the CEO's young daughter read the first chapter and demanded more.
slides.stock_query: child reading book cinematic uplifting real people

slides.heading: One Small Advance
slides.narration: Rowling was told to get a day job — children's books don't make money.
slides.stock_query: empty bookshelf cinematic uplifting real people

slides.heading: The Turn
slides.body: Word of mouth changed the trajectory.
slides.narration: Kids passed the book to friends. Teachers read it aloud. Libraries couldn't keep it on shelves.
slides.stock_query: children reading library cinematic uplifting real people

slides.heading: 500 Million Copies Later
slides.narration: Every rejection letter was, in hindsight, one publisher missing the biggest story of the decade.
slides.stock_query: stack of books cinematic uplifting real people

slides.heading: What Rejection Really Means
slides.narration: A no is not a judgment on your worth — it's information about that one door. Keep knocking.
slides.stock_query: sunrise mountain cinematic uplifting real people

slides.heading: Your Thirty-First Yes
slides.narration: The only way to guarantee no is to stop asking. Somewhere out there, your yes is waiting.
slides.stock_query: open door light cinematic uplifting real people

script.cta: If this reminded you why you started — hit subscribe. Tomorrow: another real story.
